danger_signals.visual_form: Monster sprites with hostile appearance (distinct from good monsters)
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat - will attack player on sight

danger_signals.visual_form: MF stat at zero or near-zero on character screen
danger_signals.meaning: World state danger - frequent traps spawn across the map

danger_signals.visual_form: Degraded character stats visible on status screen; slower combat animations
danger_signals.meaning: Consequence of failing to eat or sleep - character in weakened state

danger_signals.visual_form: Character movement speed noticeably reduced when Load Capacity exceeded
danger_signals.meaning: Mechanical danger - slower movement makes player vulnerable to enemy encounters

safety_signals.visual_form: Monster sprites with passive or benign appearance (visually distinct from evil variants)
safety_signals.meaning: Non-threatening unless attacked first - safe to avoid combat

safety_signals.visual_form: MF stat above 100 on character screen
safety_signals.meaning: World blessing state - random beneficial items appear in the environment

safety_signals.visual_form: Full HP bar, normal stat values, character moves at normal speed
safety_signals.meaning: Character is in optimal operational state
Safe zones for rest, trading, and information gathering

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Beneficial items appear when MF is above 100; rewards for moral choices

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Evil monsters (hostile appearance, attack on sight)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Traps spawn when MF drops to zero; punitive environmental changes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Explosion of flames near City of the Woods; monsters spread throughout world
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased hostile monster encounters; general world instability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Random beneficial items appear in environment/world exploration
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Good monsters remain passive; fewer traps; world feels rewarding

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Zero/Low Moral Fiber (evil-aligned or amoral player)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Frequent visible trap markers/hazards appearing across map
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased environmental dangers; world becomes actively hostile

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Screen darkens; visual distinction from daytime areas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Character must seek shelter/sleep; fatigue mechanics activate; different monster activity patterns possible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character sprite may show degraded animation; stat screen shows reduced HP/attack
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Movement is slower; attack power reduced; increased vulnerability to damage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character sprite moves visibly slower; visual 'weighted down' movement animation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reduced movement speed; tactical vulnerability during encounters

environmental_storytelling.example: Good vs evil monsters visually encoded; their presence tells story of moral corruption spreading post-explosion
Trap density increases based on player's moral choices (MF stat); world physically responds to morality
Day-night cycles, hunger, sleep requirements communicate a living, demanding world rather than abstract game space
Every item having physical weight tells story that this is a grounded, realistic world with physical consequences

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Remains passive; does not engage player
Becomes hostile; engages in combat; player loses Moral Fiber on kill
Immediately attacks on sight; no negotiation possible
HP and attack power gradually decrease; movement may become sluggish
Movement speed visibly reduced; tactical disadvantage in combat
Traps spawn frequently across map; punitive world transformation
Beneficial items randomly appear; rewarding world transformation

notes: Super Hydlide uses a sophisticated morality system as its primary world-state communication mechanism. Unlike Hydlide II, NPC reactions are no longer affected by alignment, but instead the physical environment responds (traps vs rewards). The game employs a dual-layer danger system: (1) monster-level threats (good vs evil designation), and (2) environmental hazards triggered by player behavior. Survival mechanics (hunger, fatigue, encumbrance) serve as constant visual/behavioral world-state indicators. The visual distinction between good and evil monsters is critical but not fully detailed in available sources. The game's innovative Moral Fiber system creates emergent environmental storytelling where the world literally changes based on cumulative player choices.
